chunk_summary: This section provides a structured template for counselling caregivers on complementary feeding for children aged 6–24 months, focusing on texture progression, meal schedules, dietary diversity, energy anchors, and food safety guidelines.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of a written complementary feeding prescription for caregivers.
Learn how to assess and set goals for texture progression in complementary feeding.
Identify key dietary diversity groups and strategies to meet Minimum Dietary Diversity (MDD).
Recognize food safety and choking prevention measures for children aged 6–24 months.
key_facts: Texture progression includes thin liquids, smooth puree, mashed, minced-chopped, soft finger foods, and family foods.
Meals should be limited to ≤30 minutes with water provided between meals and grazing avoided.
Minimum Dietary Diversity (MDD) is met if ≥5 out of 8 food groups are consumed daily.
Energy anchors include ~1 tsp oil/ghee per bowl, iron-rich foods paired with vitamin C, and calcium sources like milk or paneer.
Food safety measures include handwashing, safe water, cooking fresh, reheating food until very hot, and avoiding bottle/teat use.
reference_quote: Use a one-page 'prescription' so counselling is reproducible and the caregiver leaves with written goals.
